verses.text: Paul stretches his hand, glad to speak before one expert in Jewish questions. Courteous opening. He honours the king he addresses.
Paul stretches his hand, glad to speak before one expert in Jewish questions. Courteous opening. He honours the king he addresses.
Paul stretches his hand, glad to speak before one expert in Jewish questions. Courteous opening. He honours the king he addresses.
I lived a Pharisee; I am judged for the hope of the promise; why incredible that God should raise the dead? The true issue. The resurrection is the heart of his faith.
I lived a Pharisee; I am judged for the hope of the promise; why incredible that God should raise the dead? The true issue. The resurrection is the heart of his faith.

I lived a Pharisee; I am judged for the hope of the promise; why incredible that God should raise the dead? The true issue. The resurrection is the heart of his faith.
I thought I ought to do many things against the name of Jesus, persecuting the saints. Honest confession. He hides nothing of his former rage.
I thought I ought to do many things against the name of Jesus, persecuting the saints. Honest confession. He hides nothing of his former rage.
I thought I ought to do many things against the name of Jesus, persecuting the saints. Honest confession. He hides nothing of his former rage.
Near Damascus a light above the sun; 'Saul... why persecutest thou me? I am Jesus.' The great change. The risen Lord arrested him.
Near Damascus a light above the sun; 'Saul... why persecutest thou me? I am Jesus.' The great change. The risen Lord arrested him.

Near Damascus a light above the sun; 'Saul... why persecutest thou me? I am Jesus.' The great change. The risen Lord arrested him.
Rise, I make thee a minister, to open their eyes, 'from darkness to light.' His commission. He is sent to turn men to God.
Rise, I make thee a minister, to open their eyes, 'from darkness to light.' His commission. He is sent to turn men to God.
Rise, I make thee a minister, to open their eyes, 'from darkness to light.' His commission. He is sent to turn men to God.
I was 'not disobedient unto the heavenly vision'; for this the Jews seized me. Faithful obedience. His arrest sprang from obeying Christ.
I was 'not disobedient unto the heavenly vision'; for this the Jews seized me. Faithful obedience. His arrest sprang from obeying Christ.
I was 'not disobedient unto the heavenly vision'; for this the Jews seized me. Faithful obedience. His arrest sprang from obeying Christ.
I witness that Christ should suffer and rise, 'to shew light unto the people, and to the Gentiles.' The gospel summed. Suffering, rising, and light for all.
I witness that Christ should suffer and rise, 'to shew light unto the people, and to the Gentiles.' The gospel summed. Suffering, rising, and light for all.
Festus cries, thou art mad; 'I... speak forth the words of truth and soberness.' Calm reply. He answers the scoff with quiet reason.
Festus cries, thou art mad; 'I... speak forth the words of truth and soberness.' Calm reply. He answers the scoff with quiet reason.
Festus cries, thou art mad; 'I... speak forth the words of truth and soberness.' Calm reply. He answers the scoff with quiet reason.
'Almost thou persuadest me to be a Christian'; would all were such as I, save these bonds. Earnest longing. He wishes all his hearers saved.

This man might have been freed had he not appealed to Caesar. Innocent. Even the king owns he had done no wrong.
